Refactor the authentication middleware to use JWT tokens instead of session cookies.

Current implementation (internal/auth/middleware.go):
- Uses gorilla/sessions for session storage
- Session data stored in Redis with 24h TTL
- Cookie-based: sets "session_id" cookie on login

Problems:
- Stateful: requires Redis availability for every request
- Cannot scale horizontally without shared session store
- Mobile clients struggle with cookie-based auth

Target implementation:
- Replace session middleware with JWT validation
- Use RS256 signing (not HS256) for public key distribution
- Store only: user_id, roles, exp in JWT claims
- Refresh token endpoint: POST /auth/refresh (returns new JWT)
- Logout: client-side only (blacklist not needed for MVP)

Files affected:
- internal/auth/middleware.go (rewrite)
- internal/auth/login_handler.go (update to return JWT)
- internal/auth/jwt_keys.go (new file: key loading)
- cmd/server/main.go (remove Redis session init)
- go.mod (add golang-jwt/jwt/v5, remove gorilla/sessions)
